seoTitle: Bennett Springs vs Beresford — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Bennett Springs and Beresford. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Bennett Springs vs Beresford: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Bennett Springs and Beresford.
heading: Bennett Springs vs Beresford — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Bennett Springs, Western Australia and Beresford, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Beresford has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Bennett Springs (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Bennett Springs or Beresford safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Bennett Springs has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Beresford scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Bennett Springs scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Beresford scores 48/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Bennett Springs or Beresford?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Beresford has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
